title: CommsDraft
description: Output of Agent: comms-drafter. A drafted communication (email, Procore message, formal letter, internal note) for human review and human send. Extends pm_artifact_base. The agent NEVER sends; it only drafts. The runtime queues the draft for tier-2 (or tier-3 for owner-facing) approval; sending is an explicit human action downstream.

subject.description: Subject line (or formal-letter 're:') for the draft. ≤140 chars to keep inbox previews intact.

body_markdown.description: Body of the draft as GitHub-flavored Markdown. Mirrors the artifact's top-level body_markdown so the Documents tab can render it as the message preview without re-parsing.

recipient_class.description: Recipient class. 'owner' forces tier-3 (Charles approves before any owner-facing copy moves). 'partner' is tier-2 with external-distribution flag. The runtime reads this for routing.

tone.description: Tone of the draft. 'formal' = letter voice, third-person where appropriate. 'direct' = plain English, no fluff. 'friendly' = warm but still professional. Threatening / hostile tones are not permitted; the agent refuses such requests.

recommended_channel.description: Recommended channel for the human to use when sending. The agent drafts the message; channel is a recommendation, not a delivery action.

recommended_sender_role.description: Role label of the recommended sender (e.g. 'Project Director', 'Procurement Lead', 'GC Superintendent'). Use a person's name only when the input explicitly named them.

tone_notes.description: Short note for the reviewer on tone choices: why this register, why this length, any phrases the reviewer should consider editing for context the draft cannot infer.

contains_commitment.description: True when the draft contains language that could reasonably be read as a commitment (delivery date, cost cap, scope guarantee, warranty extension). When true, the runtime automatically attaches a 'legal_review_required' flag and the draft cannot move past tier-2 without explicit human authorization.

recipient_name.description: Optional recipient name; null if the input gave only a role.

recipient_role.description: Optional recipient role label (e.g. 'Owner Construction Lead'). Null if the input did not provide one.

purpose.description: Verbatim copy of the purpose from the input. Lets reviewers quickly see what the draft is meant to accomplish.

key_facts_used.description: Subset of input key_facts the draft relies on. The reviewer can confirm at a glance that the draft is grounded in what the team provided and didn't invent material.

redactions.description: Items the agent redacted or omitted from the draft (PII, safety-sensitive content, attorney-client material). The runtime adds 'pii_redacted' or 'safety' to escalation_reasons as appropriate.

channel_hint_honored.description: True when the input provided a channel_hint and the agent honored it; false when the agent chose a different channel (and explained why in tone_notes); null when no hint was provided.
